warnings: The pipeline is usable but dominated by high-severity uncontrolled theory and phenomenology assumptions.
The librarian, critic/model, PTA, and constraints handoffs all carry warning status.
No new physics calculation, scan, or constraint recast was performed by the prior agent.
No targeted web fetch was needed because the upstream librarian and constraint handoffs already document the relevant literature and missing observables.
approximations: Severity scores are qualitative audit rankings on the schema's 1-10 scale.
Controlled means the item is explicitly handled well enough for the existing pipeline output; it does not imply the physics assumption is universally validated.
The audit prioritizes interpretation risk for the completed fopt-pta workflow rather than proposing new benchmark points.

upstream_statuses.summary: No exact no-fermion, charge-one, PTArcade-ready match was found; closest benchmarks require convention and model-content translation.
Model is internally consistent for the semi-analytic backend, but portal values, kinetic mixing, thermalization, gauge dependence, and full thermal masses remain warnings.
Authoritative model uses independent v and g_D, dependent lambda_Phi, fixed q_Phi=1 and xi=1, and negligible portal/kinetic-mixing effects for FOPT.
Semi-analytic conformal single-field scan executed with 79 viable points out of 120, but uses high-temperature and percolation approximations.
PTA benchmark and PTArcade campaign completed, but template choice, wall dynamics, and convergence diagnostics remain limited.
Only theory consistency constraints were directly applied; most laboratory and cosmology constraints require new observables or recasts.

assumptions.description: The broad class of classically conformal dark U(1) phase transitions has already been studied; novelty is only partial for the exact no-fermion, charge-one, PTArcade implementation.
assumptions.required_solution: Frame novelty narrowly and compare against the closest U(1) Coleman-Weinberg/PTA papers with explicit model-content differences.

source.note: Not novel as a broad class; partially novel only in the stripped-down convention and implementation.

assumptions.description: Literature benchmarks use multiple U(1) charge and vev conventions, including m_ZD = 2 g_D M, while this pipeline uses q_Phi = 1 and m_Aprime = g_D v.
assumptions.required_solution: Maintain an explicit conversion table for each imported benchmark and verify all mass, vev, and coupling translations before quoting comparisons.

source.note: Closest same-model paper uses a different dark charge/mass convention.

assumptions.description: lambda_Phi is fixed by the Coleman-Weinberg/backend relation and is not an independent scanned parameter.
assumptions.required_solution: Keep lambda_Phi out of independent priors and record beta_lambda/lambda_Phi as derived quantities for every posterior sample.

source.note: The critic and FOPT handoffs consistently treat lambda_Phi as dependent.

assumptions.description: epsilon and lambda_HP are unspecified and assumed negligible for FOPT, but they are essential for laboratory, cosmological, and visible-sector constraints.
assumptions.required_solution: Specify priors or benchmark values for epsilon and lambda_HP and implement the corresponding width, lifetime, branching-ratio, and production calculations.

source.note: No collider, beam-dump, SN1987A, BBN, CMB, direct, or indirect exclusion was applied without these observables.

assumptions.description: The FOPT/PTA calculation fixes xi = T_dark/T_visible = 1 while the couplings that could maintain thermal contact are small or unspecified.
assumptions.required_solution: Compute Gamma_SM-dark/H, decoupling, entropy transfer, and xi(T), or explicitly impose and justify xi = 1 as an initial condition.

source.note: xi=1 is an input assumption, not a derived result.

assumptions.description: The FOPT stage uses the semi-analytic conformal single-field backend with a high-temperature/small-field polynomial form and subleading scalar/Goldstone treatment.
assumptions.required_solution: Benchmark against a full finite-temperature effective-potential implementation over representative posterior points.

source.note: Semi-analytic backend with scalar/Goldstone contributions treated as subleading.

assumptions.description: Full Daisy resummation, RG running, longitudinal gauge thermal masses, scalar thermal masses, and gauge/resummation prescription variations were not implemented.
assumptions.required_solution: Add a controlled finite-temperature potential implementation and compare Landau-gauge, gauge-independent, and resummation-scheme variants where available.

source.note: Not required by the selected backend, but required for a full finite-temperature implementation.

assumptions.description: The backend uses a Gaussian false-vacuum-fraction/percolation approximation; percolation and eternal-inflation checks are present only at backend-screen level.
assumptions.required_solution: Run posterior-level percolation and false-vacuum survival checks with uncertainty bands for the preferred region.

source.note: Percolation_checked is true, but the method remains approximate.

assumptions.description: The PTA-preferred points involve very large alpha, sometimes extremely large, so standard beta/H, reheating, and fluid approximations may not be reliably controlled.
assumptions.required_solution: Audit posterior samples for supercooling validity, reheating consistency, vacuum domination, and beta/H definition robustness.

source.note: Best 14/14 benchmark has alpha about 2.1e6; another has alpha about 2.1e11.

assumptions.description: Wall velocity, microscopic friction, runaway conditions, and the bulk-flow/dissipative-bulk-flow partition were not computed.
assumptions.required_solution: Implement a wall-dynamics module or scan conservative wall/friction regimes and propagate the uncertainty through the PTA spectrum.

source.note: bf was selected because dissipative wall-friction inputs are absent.

assumptions.description: Published finite-temperature comparisons and related benchmarks often use Landau-gauge one-loop potentials; gauge dependence remains an uncontrolled theory systematic.
assumptions.required_solution: Compare against gauge-independent approximations or vary gauge/resummation prescriptions to estimate a theory envelope.

source.note: Gauge dependence of finite-temperature effective potentials is flagged across relevant papers.

assumptions.description: The bf spectrum template is the closest implemented template for a strongly supercooled transition, not a first-principles validation of this model's GW source.
assumptions.required_solution: Propagate template alternatives and wall-dynamics uncertainty, and avoid interpreting the bf posterior as uniquely model-selected.

source.note: Closest-template choice with caveats.

assumptions.description: The higgsless sound-wave template was considered but not selected because the large-alpha supercooled regime is outside clean sound-wave validity assumptions.
assumptions.required_solution: Keep the sound-wave score as a diagnostic only unless a validity calculation supports it.

source.note: higgsless scored bins but was not selected for physical validity reasons.

assumptions.description: PTArcade completed one 100000-sample campaign with finite chains, but no multi-chain convergence, autocorrelation, effective-sample-size, or prior-sensitivity study was performed.
assumptions.required_solution: Run independent chains, inspect autocorrelation and effective sample size, and repeat with alternative priors/templates.

source.note: Acceptance and finite rows were checked, but convergence was not.

assumptions.description: PTArcade samples v through a log-uniform prior on log10_v over 0.003-5 GeV and g_D through a uniform prior over 0.42-1.05; these choices are informative.
assumptions.required_solution: Run prior-range and measure-sensitivity checks, including wider/narrower v and g_D ranges and explicit reporting of boundary posterior mass.

source.note: v prior is uniform in log10_v; g_D prior is uniform linearly.

assumptions.description: The FOPT seed scan covered v = 0.01-1.0 GeV and g_D = 0.1-0.9, while the PTA/PTArcade prior later expanded to v = 0.003-5.0 GeV and g_D = 0.42-1.05.
assumptions.required_solution: Document why the later PTA range is valid and run a consistent posterior-driven FOPT diagnostic over the full PTArcade prior box.

source.note: PTA stage expanded the explored range around viable benchmarks.

assumptions.description: f_peak_est in the FOPT scan is only an order-of-magnitude redshifted frequency scale setter and not the physical PTA template peak.
assumptions.required_solution: Continue using full PTA spectra for likelihood work and keep f_peak_est out of final spectral claims.

source.note: The handoff explicitly warns that PTA spectral templates belong to the PTA stage.

assumptions.description: Most experimental, astrophysical, and cosmological constraints were not applied because required observables were missing.
assumptions.required_solution: Treat the current result as unconstrained by those searches until the missing observables and recasts are implemented.

source.note: Only 2 constraints were directly testable; 11 require a new backend.

assumptions.description: A_prime widths, visible/invisible branching fractions, decay lengths, production rates, and recasts are missing because epsilon and decay assumptions are absent.
assumptions.required_solution: Implement dark_photon_widths.py and dark_photon_recasts.py with an epsilon prior and decay-channel model.

source.note: Dark photon limits cannot be applied from mass alone.

assumptions.description: rho decay widths, Higgs mixing, portal production, and scalar lifetimes are missing; rho -> A_prime A_prime is closed in the PTArcade 1sigma box.
assumptions.required_solution: Implement lambda_HP to scalar-Higgs mixing, rho partial widths, and cosmological lifetime/yield calculations.

source.note: Visible rho decays are portal controlled.

assumptions.description: BBN, CMB, Delta N_eff, freeze-in, residual abundance, and late energy-injection constraints are not computed.
assumptions.required_solution: Implement thermal_history.py, cosmology_constraints.py, and neff.py with portal couplings, decay tables, yields, and entropy tracking.

source.note: Masses and xi=1 are insufficient to apply cosmology bounds.

assumptions.description: The constraints agent treated the PTArcade 1sigma region as a rectangular box rather than reconstructing correlated posterior samples.
assumptions.required_solution: Propagate actual chain samples through derived masses and future constraint backends.

source.note: Parameter correlations were not reconstructed from the chain.

assumptions.description: Direct-detection constraints are not applicable to the validated minimal no-fermion model because no stable dark matter particle or local density fraction is defined.
assumptions.required_solution: Do not quote direct-detection bounds unless a stable dark matter candidate is added.

source.note: Direct detection can be reopened only if a DM candidate is added.

assumptions.description: Closest PTA-scale conformal U(1) benchmarks include additional fermions, dark matter dynamics, or different thermal histories that can shift thermal masses and viable priors.
assumptions.required_solution: Separate same-model, structurally similar, and target-signal-only benchmarks in all reports and avoid transferring priors without caveats.

source.note: arXiv:2502.19478 is close but includes anomaly-cancelling fermions and dark matter dynamics.

assumptions.description: The FOPT f_peak_est uses g_* interpolated from a table and clamped at table boundaries.
assumptions.required_solution: For final spectra, use the PTA template computation; if f_peak_est is reported, include the g_* interpolation/clamping caveat.

source.note: g_* for f_peak_est is interpolated and clamped.

assumptions.description: The PTArcade handoff reports posterior summaries but no evidence estimate or evidence error, so model comparison support is absent.
assumptions.required_solution: Run an evidence-capable campaign or restrict claims to posterior fits under the chosen model/template/prior.

source.note: evidence and evidence_error are null.

required_followups.description: Specify epsilon and lambda_HP priors or benchmarks and build dark photon/dark Higgs width, lifetime, branching-ratio, and portal-mixing calculations.
required_followups.suggested_action: Implement dark_photon_widths.py and dark_higgs_portal.py; validate against standard leptonic widths, hadronic R-ratio treatment, and light-scalar partial widths.

required_followups.description: Check whether xi = 1 is dynamically consistent with the assumed small or unspecified portals.
required_followups.suggested_action: Implement thermal_history.py to compute Gamma_SM-dark/H, decoupling, entropy transfer, and xi(T) across the posterior.

required_followups.description: Apply BBN, CMB, Delta N_eff, freeze-in, and late-decay energy-injection constraints once decay tables and yields exist.
required_followups.suggested_action: Implement cosmology_constraints.py and neff.py and propagate posterior samples rather than a rectangular 1sigma box.

required_followups.description: Quantify FOPT theory uncertainty from the semi-analytic backend and finite-temperature approximations.
required_followups.suggested_action: Compare representative posterior points against a full finite-temperature calculation with Daisy/RG/gauge-prescription variations and posterior-level percolation checks.

required_followups.description: Compute wall dynamics or bracket wall-friction/runaway regimes before treating the bf template posterior as physically robust.
required_followups.suggested_action: Add wall-friction/runaway diagnostics and propagate bf/dbf/sound-wave template uncertainty through PTA spectra.

required_followups.description: Repeat PTArcade with convergence diagnostics and prior/template sensitivity studies.
required_followups.suggested_action: Run multiple independent chains, compute autocorrelation/effective sample size, inspect boundary posterior mass, and record evidence if model comparison is required.

required_followups.description: Align FOPT scan coverage with the final PTArcade prior volume and propagate derived lambda_Phi/beta_lambda for every sample.
required_followups.suggested_action: Run a posterior-grid diagnostic over v = 0.003-5 GeV and g_D = 0.42-1.05, while keeping lambda_Phi dependent.

required_followups.description: Apply laboratory dark-photon and light-scalar constraints after FUP01 provides lifetimes and branching fractions.
required_followups.suggested_action: Implement BaBar, LHCb, beam-dump, NA64/missing-energy, and light-scalar recasts with experiment-specific acceptances.

required_followups.description: Create a benchmark-convention ledger for all imported literature points.
required_followups.suggested_action: For each cited benchmark, record q_Phi, vev convention, vector mass formula, fermion content, thermal history, and resummation/gauge prescription.

required_followups.description: Only reopen dark-matter direct and indirect searches if the model is extended with a stable or long-lived dark matter candidate.
required_followups.suggested_action: Keep direct-detection constraints marked not applicable for the current minimal no-fermion model.
